description: Schema for theorem/property trust labels assigned to proof artifacts. Each label indicates the level of confidence in a verified property and the conditions under which it holds.

EXHAUSTIVE_FINITE_PROOF.description: Property verified by exhaustive enumeration over all inputs within finite bounds.
EXHAUSTIVE_FINITE_PROOF.applicability: All inputs satisfying the stated size/complexity bounds

artifact_path.description: Path to the proof artifact file

checker_command.description: Command to re-run the proof verification

bounds.description: Finite bounds within which the proof is exhaustive

properties.max_domain_size: integer: maximum size of the domain
properties.max_term_depth: integer: maximum nesting depth of terms
properties.enumeration_count: integer: total number of cases checked

assumptions.description: List of assumptions required for the proof to hold

limitations.description: List of limitations: what the proof does NOT cover

proof_strategy.description: Description of the proof approach (exhaustive enumeration, brute-force search, etc.)

example.label: EXHAUSTIVE_FINITE_PROOF
limitations: Does not extend to infinite domains
Does not cover domains with |D| > 4 or |Atom| > 4
example.proof_strategy: Exhaustive enumeration of all possible Galois connections within bounds

SMT_PROVED_FINITE.description: Property verified by SMT solver for a finite (possibly parameterized) scope.
SMT_PROVED_FINITE.applicability: All inputs within the SMT model's scope

artifact_path.description: Path to the SMT2 or Python/Z3 proof artifact

checker_command.description: Command to invoke the SMT solver

smt_solver.description: Name and version of the SMT solver used

model_scope.description: Description of the finite scope modeled in SMT

LEAN_PROVED.description: Property formally proven in the Lean theorem prover with full mathematical rigor.
LEAN_PROVED.applicability: All inputs satisfying the theorem preconditions (potentially infinite)

artifact_path.description: Path to the Lean source file

checker_command.description: Command to compile/check the Lean proof

axioms_used.description: List of axioms/imports the proof depends on

TLA_PROVED.description: Property verified using the TLA+ model checker (TLC).
TLA_PROVED.applicability: All behaviors within the modeled state space

artifact_path.description: Path to the TLA+ spec file

tla_version.description: Version of TLA+ Toolbox or TLC

invariants_checked.description: List of invariants verified by TLC

REFUTED.description: Property has been disproven with a concrete counterexample.
REFUTED.applicability: The property is false; counterexample demonstrates the failure

counterexample_id.description: Reference to the counterexample registry entry

violated_property.description: Formal statement of the property that was violated

assumptions.description: Assumptions under which the property was tested

limitations.description: Scope of the refutation (may not generalize beyond tested scope)

PENDING_TOOLCHAIN.description: Proof artifact exists but cannot be checked due to missing toolchain.
PENDING_TOOLCHAIN.applicability: Proof status cannot be determined without the required tool

checker_command.description: Command that would check the proof if toolchain were available

missing_toolchain.description: Name of the required but unavailable tool

assumptions.description: Assumed properties if the proof were to succeed

fallback_evidence.description: Alternative evidence for the property (e.g., Python exhaustive proof, manual review)

PARTIAL_PROOF.description: Property partially proven; some cases verified but not all.

checker_command.description: Command to run the partial proof

coverage_description.description: Description of what cases are covered

MANUAL_REVIEW.description: Property verified by expert manual review without automated checking.

reviewer.description: Name or identifier of the reviewer

status_transitions.description: Valid transitions between trust label statuses

transitions.trigger: Toolchain becomes available and proof is checked
Remaining cases are proven or a counterexample is found
Automated proof or refutation is completed

severity_levels.CRITICAL: Property failure causes security/privacy breach or data corruption
severity_levels.HIGH: Property failure causes significant correctness or reliability issue
severity_levels.MEDIUM: Property failure causes moderate deviation from expected behavior
severity_levels.LOW: Property failure has minimal impact; documented limitation acceptable
severity_levels.INFO: Informational only; no failure but worth documenting
